allowed_change_scope.scope: The join-request rumor callback passed to startWelcomeSubscription (the 5th argument, around line 811). Add a setPendingRequests state append alongside the existing incrementJoinRequest call. No other function, callback, or state in this file may change.

New e2e spec file (does not previously exist). Reproduces the bug: admin stays on an already-open group detail page while a peer joins via invite link; asserts both the live bell update and the live pending-requests-section update.

constraints: No refactoring of the dispatcher, the welcome-subscription module, or the join-request handler/storage layer.
No version-counter or new synchronization primitive — a plain setPendingRequests state append is the entire fix.
No changes to the working invite-by-npub / commit path (onMembersChanged and its consumers) — that path already refreshes live via the shared groups state and must remain untouched.
No protocol or relay-publish changes. No new public broadcast of any kind — this is purely in-app reactive state wiring (privacy invariant unaffected).
Fix must be idempotent under both event orderings: mount effect (loadPendingRequestsForGroup, IDB read) running before or after the live callback. Dedup by eventId.
Do not touch PendingRequestsSection.tsx, joinRequestHandler.ts, or joinRequestStorage.ts — the bug is purely that MarmotContext never forwards the already-persisted request into React state.
non_goals: No refactor of the dual-listener architecture (MarmotContext + ChatStoreContext) described in specs/marmot-application-rumor-dispatch.md.
No change to how the notification bell counter works (incrementJoinRequest/decrementJoinRequest) — it already behaves correctly and is left untouched.
No attempt to also live-refresh other pending-request-adjacent surfaces (e.g. groups list badge count) beyond what the existing bell + section already cover.
No new e2e test infra, docker config, or helper file — reuse existing bootUser/createGroup/openGroupDetail patterns from groups-invite-link.spec.ts.

baseline.lint: NO_LINT — no ESLint config exists in app/ (next lint prompts interactively to scaffold one; project has never configured it, pre-existing condition, out of scope for a surgical bug fix)
